cuisine_type: Southeast Asian (Thai / Malaysian / Singaporean / Indonesian) — contemporary playful
address: 武定路966号，近常德路 / 966 Wuding Lu, near Changde Lu, Jing'an
head_chef: Jun Nishiyama (西山 純) — Japanese-Singaporean, formerly senior sous chef at Ultraviolet by Paul Pairet and chef-owner of Sage Gastro (closed 2025-12-31). Partnered with Alice Fan (ex-Sage), Tiger (ex-Ambra), Chad (Bar Choice / Man Tang).
signature_dishes: Rendang Donut ¥68 — beef oxtail with chocolate mole in Guinness-glazed bao
Oxtail rendang doughnut with kecap manis
Ayam penyet with turmeric aioli & pineapple sambal
Soft shell crab ¥98 with laksa lemak & crispy wonton skins
Ikan Bakar ¥238 — whole grilled turbot with sambal butter
Nasi Ulam ¥58 — herb rice with crispy salted fish & chicken skin
vibe_and_space: Mid-sized Wuding Lu space, ~3 dozen seats. Baby-blue and pandan-green tables, bright yellow wavy bar, batik wall pieces, congkak boards as decor. Intimate yet communal — a deliberate visual pivot away from Sage Gastro's refined fine-dining setting. Casual à-la-carte rather than tasting-menu format.
one_paragraph_recommendation: The chef-lineage successor to Sage Gastro — Jun Nishiyama's deliberate reset from refined fine-dining tasting menu (Sage Gastro, closed Dec 31 2025) to a casual à-la-carte Southeast Asian playground. Same chef pedigree (ex-Ultraviolet senior sous chef), but a totally different format: per-dish pricing ¥24-238, three dozen seats, batik-and-pastel space. Editorial reviews flag this as a 'reinterpret-rather-than-recreate' approach — Rendang Donut, laksa soft-shell crab, sambal-butter turbot read as Nishiyama's Japanese-Singaporean training applied to SEA flavors with creative liberty. Recommend for friends or a casual date if you want chef-driven cooking without the tasting-menu commitment; it's also the canonical worked example of the 'closure → casual reincarnation' pattern visible in 2025-26 Shanghai (vs the 'closure → next fine-dining concept' pattern). Track 大众点评 ratings post-soft-opening; current authority signal is editorial-only.
contradictions: Chef pedigree (Ultraviolet 3-star background + Sage Gastro editorial favorite) suggests fine-dining quality, but Rambu's deliberately casual format prevents direct authority-signal carryover. The interesting question: does a Michelin-track chef's casual concept inherit any of that signal, or does it have to re-prove itself on 大众点评 / 公众号 from scratch? No data yet.
